evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan AR to my React Native app. Here's my screen file: EmptyApp.tsx. I need to track EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and show a circle highlight on each one.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeAr* integration code into EmptyApp.tsx as a function component with hooks, shows a BarcodeArCircleHighlight provider, and shows the setup checklist (packages to install, npx pod-install, camera permission entries).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-react-native-datacapture-core and scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode)
Setup checklist mentions npx pod-install for iOS
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription in ios Info.plist
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API used)
DataCaptureContext.sharedInstance is present (semantic)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
new BarcodeAr(settings) is present (constructor pattern, not a static factory)
dataCaptureContext.addMode( is present
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
<BarcodeArView is in the JSX tree (semantic)
BarcodeArView has a context prop and a barcodeAr prop (semantic)
view.start() is present
view.start() is invoked inside the BarcodeArView ref callback (semantic)
highlightProvider prop is passed to BarcodeArView
BarcodeArCircleHighlight is constructed inside highlightForBarcode
highlightForBarcode returns a Promise (semantic)
The component is written as a function component (no class syntax)
useRef is used to hold the BarcodeAr mode reference
useEffect cleanup calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(...)
Import from 'scandit-react-native-datacapture-core' is present
Import from 'scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode' is present

evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan AR barcode scanning to ProductListApp.tsx. When a barcode is added to the tracked set, append it to the scannedProducts state and re-render the product list. Keep the existing clear button and list rendering.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeAr* integration to ProductListApp.tsx. The existing scannedProducts state, render logic, and clear button are preserved. Newly tracked barcodes from session.addedTrackedBarcodes are appended via setScannedProducts.

assertions.text: DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
new BarcodeAr(settings) is present
dataCaptureContext.addMode( is present
<BarcodeArView is rendered in the component's JSX
view.start() is present
view.start() is invoked inside the BarcodeArView ref callback (semantic)
didUpdateSession( is present
session.addedTrackedBarcodes is accessed to get newly detected barcodes
setScannedProducts is called to append to the product list
Existing scannedProducts state and setScannedProducts setter are preserved
Existing FlatList rendering is preserved
Existing clear button / onClearTapped handler is preserved
useEffect cleanup calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(...)

evals.prompt: I want to show a custom React component as the highlight for each barcode in my MatrixScan AR app instead of the built-in circle. The custom component should be a small rounded view with the barcode data text inside it.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses BarcodeArCustomHighlight with a renderHighlight function that returns a React component. The highlight provider returns a BarcodeArCustomHighlight instance. A note about SDK 8.0+ requirement is included.

assertions.text: BarcodeArCustomHighlight is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
new BarcodeArCustomHighlight({ is present
renderHighlight is a function returning a ReactElement / JSX
A React functional component or inline JSX is returned from renderHighlight
The custom component renders a View with styling (border, borderRadius, or similar)
highlightForBarcode returns a Promise (semantic)
Existing BarcodeAr mode, context initialization, and BarcodeArView JSX are preserved (semantic)
view.start() is present

evals.prompt: Add an info annotation to my MatrixScan AR app that shows when a barcode is scanned. The annotation should have a header showing the symbology name and a body row showing the barcode data. The entire annotation should be tappable.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeArAnnotationProvider with annotationForBarcode returning a BarcodeArInfoAnnotation that has a header (BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader), a body (BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent array), isEntireAnnotationTappable set to true, and a listener with a didTap callback.

assertions.text: BarcodeArAnnotationProvider is used (annotationProvider prop on BarcodeArView)
annotationForBarcode( is present
new BarcodeArInfoAnnotation( is present
new BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader() is present
new BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent() is present
annotation.isEntireAnnotationTappable = true is present
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationListener is implemented with a didTap callback
annotation.listener is present
BarcodeArInfoAnnotation is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
Existing highlight provider and BarcodeAr mode setup are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: I need proper cleanup for my MatrixScan AR screen — when the screen unmounts, the BarcodeAr mode should be unbound from the context so the camera and native resources are released.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds or verifies dataCaptureContext.removeMode(barcodeArRef.current) inside the useEffect cleanup function (the function returned from useEffect). dataCaptureContext.dispose() must not be called.

assertions.text: useEffect cleanup (returned function from useEffect) calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(...)
The BarcodeAr mode passed to removeMode is the one held in the useRef (barcodeArRef.current or equivalent)
dataCaptureContext.dispose() is NOT present
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr mode, BarcodeArView JSX, highlight provider, annotation provider) is preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR app, change the circle highlight so it uses a custom brush — a semi-transparent green fill with a solid green 2dp stroke — and put an icon inside each circle. File: IntegratedApp.tsx
evals.expected_output: The skill sets a custom Brush on the BarcodeArCircleHighlight via the highlight.brush property (fill, stroke, stroke width) and assigns a ScanditIcon (built with ScanditIconBuilder().withIcon(...).build()) to highlight.icon. Brush, Color, ScanditIconBuilder, and ScanditIconType are imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core.

assertions.text: highlight.brush is assigned a new Brush instance
new Brush( is constructed with a fill color, a stroke color, and a stroke width
Color.fromHex( is used to build the brush colors
highlight.icon is assigned a ScanditIcon
the icon is built with new ScanditIconBuilder().withIcon( and .build()
withIcon is called with a valid ScanditIconType member (semantic)
Brush is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core
ScanditIconBuilder is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core
BarcodeArCircleHighlight is constructed inside highlightForBarcode (semantic)
Existing BarcodeAr mode, context initialization, and BarcodeArView JSX are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add a status icon annotation to my MatrixScan AR app. Each scanned barcode should show a compact warning icon, and when tapped it should expand to show the text 'Low stock'. Use a yellow background. File: IntegratedApp.tsx
evals.expected_output: The skill returns a BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation from annotationForBarcode, sets its icon to a ScanditIcon built via ScanditIconBuilder().withIcon(ScanditIconType.ExclamationMark).build(), sets text to 'Low stock', and sets a backgroundColor. BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation is imported from the barcode package; ScanditIconBuilder/ScanditIconType/Color from the core package.

assertions.text: new BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode) is present
BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
statusAnnotation.icon is assigned a ScanditIcon built with new ScanditIconBuilder().withIcon( and .build()
ScanditIconBuilder is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core
statusAnnotation.text is assigned the string 'Low stock'
statusAnnotation.backgroundColor is assigned a Color
annotationForBarcode( returns the BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation (semantic)
BarcodeArAnnotationProvider is passed as the annotationProvider prop on BarcodeArView (semantic)
Existing BarcodeAr mode, context initialization, and BarcodeArView JSX are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want a popover with two action buttons to appear when the user taps a barcode highlight in my MatrixScan AR app: a 'Details' button and an 'Add' button, each with an icon. Log which button was tapped. File: IntegratedApp.tsx
evals.expected_output: The skill returns a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation built from an array of BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton instances (each constructed with a ScanditIcon built via ScanditIconBuilder and a label). A BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationListener with didTapButton is attached via popover.listener. Symbols imported from the correct packages.

assertions.text: new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, is constructed with an array of buttons
new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton( is constructed for the Details button
new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton( is constructed for the Add button
each popover button icon is built with new ScanditIconBuilder().withIcon( and .build()
the ScanditIcon is the first argument to BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton and the label string is the second (semantic)
popover.listener is assigned a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationListener
didTapButton is implemented on the popover listener
BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
ScanditIconBuilder is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core
Existing BarcodeAr mode, context initialization, and BarcodeArView JSX are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Make the annotation in my MatrixScan AR app responsive to distance: show a detailed info card with the barcode data when the barcode is close to the camera, and a compact 'Get closer' card when it is far away. Switch at 5% of the screen area. File: IntegratedApp.tsx
evals.expected_output: The skill builds two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation variants (a detailed close-up one and a compact far-away one) and wraps them in a BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation constructed with (barcode, closeUp, farAway). It sets responsive.threshold to 0.05. A note that BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation requires SDK 8.2+ is included.

assertions.text: new BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation(barcode, is constructed with a close-up annotation and a far-away annotation
two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instances are constructed as the close-up and far-away variants (semantic)
responsive.threshold is assigned the value 0.05
BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
annotationForBarcode( returns the BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation (semantic)
A note that BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation requires SDK 8.2+ is included
Existing BarcodeAr mode, context initialization, and BarcodeArView JSX are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Change the info annotation in my MatrixScan AR app so it only appears when the user taps the barcode highlight, not automatically when the barcode is scanned. File: IntegratedApp.tsx
evals.expected_output: The skill sets annotation.annotationTrigger to BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTap so the annotation shows only on highlight tap. BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger is imported from the barcode package.

assertions.text: annotation.annotationTrigger is assigned BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTap
BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTapAndBarcodeScan is NOT used for this annotation (semantic)
BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
Existing BarcodeArInfoAnnotation construction and annotation provider are preserved (semantic)
Existing BarcodeAr mode, context initialization, and BarcodeArView JSX are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR app I want to show the torch button and the camera switch button but hide the zoom button, and default to the world-facing (back) camera. File: IntegratedApp.tsx
evals.expected_output: The skill sets shouldShowTorchControl = true, shouldShowCameraSwitchControl = true, and shouldShowZoomControl = false on the BarcodeArView inside the ref callback, and sets defaultCameraPosition to CameraPosition.WorldFacing on a BarcodeArViewSettings instance passed as the settings prop.

assertions.text: view.shouldShowTorchControl = true is set in the BarcodeArView ref callback
view.shouldShowCameraSwitchControl = true is set in the BarcodeArView ref callback
view.shouldShowZoomControl = false is set in the BarcodeArView ref callback
BarcodeArViewSettings defaultCameraPosition is assigned CameraPosition.WorldFacing
CameraPosition is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core
the settings prop on BarcodeArView is a BarcodeArViewSettings instance (semantic)
view.start() is invoked inside the BarcodeArView ref callback (semantic)
Existing BarcodeAr mode, context initialization, and BarcodeArView JSX are preserved (semantic)
